message:
fix(ffs-fuse): zero-length copy_file_range bypasses read-only mutation guard

dispatch_copy_file_range previously called enforce_mutation_guards before
the early-out for len == 0, so a zero-byte copy_file_range against a
read-only mount returned EROFS even though no write would actually occur
and the kernel/glibc contract for zero-length copy_file_range is "succeed
with 0 bytes copied, touch nothing." Reordered the guard call to run only
after the zero-length short-circuit, and added regression coverage in the
ffs-fuse unit tests proving:

- dispatch_copy_file_range with len == 0 returns Ok(0) on the
  MutationRecordingFs::with_scope_recording mount that would otherwise
  refuse any RequestOp::Write.
- the recording vector stays empty, so the backend never sees a scope
  call at all, matching the "no observable side effect" contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
